programme.title: Inside Longleat
programme.desc: We step back through the gates of the world-famous Longleat Estate. Lord and Lady Bath grant unprecedented access to their 9000-acre estate as we go behind the scenes to find out what keeps their stately home, safari park and estate, running seven days a week 365 days a year. It's summer, and the carnival has arrived at Longleat. The team are working against the clock to get it open in time for the weekend, but things aren't going to plan with the helter-skelter. Bus driver Dave 'the legend' is back after his treatment for prostate cancer. He tells us how he puts his successful return down to a combination of King Charles and the NHS. And there's a big, long-awaited surprise from the team, which makes him and his guests very happy, but is it monkey-proof? Exec Prod: Jo Pearce; Series Prod: Ian Durham; Dir: Dominick French; Bright Branch Media Ltd (S2 Ep1)

programme.title: Sussex: Jewel of the South Coast
programme.desc: We celebrate one of the UK's greenest counties, showcasing the many jewels in its crown. We meet veterinary nurses who save wildlife at Mallydams Rescue Centre, witness the release of wildlife back to the Sussex shoreline, and see the inaugural locomotive launch of the Bluebell Railway. History and heritage are celebrated as we meet the oldest hop-growing family in Britain at Tibbs Farm who turn crisis into triumph by inventing a unique green hop beer. And we capture the excitement of Britain's underwater detectives as they dive the wreck of the Dutch warship Klein Hollandia in search of underwater treasure. Series Editor: Nicky Taylor; Prods/Dirs: James Bainbridge/James Nutt (Ep1)

programme.title: Prince George at 13: The Next Generation
programme.desc: Prince George is not only gearing up for a new school, he's stepping closer to the destiny that will one day see him crowned King. But what kind of monarch will he be? And could his progressive upbringing not just shape his reign, but change the monarchy forever? In this fascinating documentary, we delve into the making of a future king. Unlike William and Harry who grew up in the glare of the media and endured the very public loss of their mother, George has been shielded, protected, and raised with a level of normality no heir before him has experienced. But how are William and Kate preparing him for the moment the spotlight turns on him? Series Prod: Naomi Callan; Exec Prod: Dympna Jackson; Objective Media Group trading as Purple Productions
